column8: Brain was feeling a bit nervous about his journey. He usually enjoyed_____________, but this time was different. (TRAVEL)
column9: His mother____________him to stop worrying. "If you've checked the timetable, there's nothing to worry about", she said. (TELL)
column10: Brain knew she_______________. How could she know how worried he was? (NOT UNDERSTAND)
column11: "It's all right for her", he said to himself. "She__________________to go, but I do." (NOT HAVE)
column12: Brain realised he didn't feel very well. He decided that if he still____________bad the next morning, he wouldn't go. (FEEL)
column13: This decision made him feel a lot______________and he went to bed and slept like a baby. (GOOD)
column14: The next morning, he woke up and took the bus to his new school for the___________time. It wasn't as bad as he'd been expected. (ONE)
column15: As he walked through the gates, he knew this was the place for him. "it feels like the school is__________ ." (ME)
column16: And, in a way, the school did belong to him - because Brain______________a student. He'd just become the school's new Headteacher. (NOT BE)
column17: Do you play chess? In 1996, a chess-playing computer called Deep Blue_______________by IBM, a US computer company. (DEVELOP)
column18: The idea behind Deep Blue was to create a computer that was capable of_______________a World Chess Champion. (BEAT)
column19: The Deep Blue team______________the World Champion and Russian Grand Master, Gary Kasparov, to play series of matches against the computer. (CHALLENGE)
column20: In the first match, Kasparov beat the computer. However, in the second match in 1997, he_____________by one game, much to the shock of the watching world. (LOSE)
column21: since then, many people, including Kasparov,_______________IBM of cheating. They claim that the computer had human help during the games. (ACCUSE)
column22: And some people said that IBM did not make_______________look very good when they refused a re-match with Kasparov. (THEY)
column23: Instead, scientists at the company_____________the computer apart. (TAKE)
column24: Later, one of the developers of Big Blue___________a book saying he had been happy to built a new computer but that Kasparov wasn't interested. (WRITE)
column25: It___________possible that we will never know the full truth about what happened with Deep Blue. (BE)
